title: Boat Rental in Musandam: 2026 Guide to Fjords & Dhow Trips
html:
Musandam, Oman's dramatic fjord peninsula, offers some of the Arabian Peninsula's most spectacular boating. Departing from Khasab harbour, a half-day dhow cruise starts at around €40 per person, while private motorboat charters from €250. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators for these adventures.

Where to set off in Musandam

Most trips begin at Khasab Harbour, the main port serving the peninsula. Nearby, the Khasab Marina provides sheltered berthing for private yachts. For those exploring the southern coast, the fishing village of Dibba offers alternative launch points, particularly for trips to the Telegraph Island ruins. The bay of Khor Ash Sham is a popular stop for dolphin-watching, while the secluded beach at Kumzar marks the northern limit for many charters.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Musandam?

Prices range from €40 per person for shared dhow tours to €1,200 per day for a crewed luxury yacht. The cost depends on boat type, duration and whether crew and fuel are included.

  • Shared dhow cruise (half-day): from €40 per person
  • Small motorboat (half-day, up to 6 guests): from €300
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 12 guests): from €800
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full-day, up to 8 guests): from €1,200

To compare the best deals and rent a boat in Musandam, browse the listings on BoatHire24, where local operators publish transparent prices.

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size and desired experience. Here are the most popular options:

  1. Traditional dhow, best for sightseeing and dolphin-watching; carries up to 20 passengers; from €40 per person.
  2. Motorboat (6, 8 m), ideal for small groups wanting speed and flexibility; up to 6 guests; from €300 half-day.
  3. Catamaran (up to 40 ft), stable and spacious for day cruises; up to 12 guests; from €800 full-day.
  4. Luxury yacht (e.g., Fairline Targa 43), premium comfort for private trips; up to 8 guests; from €1,200 full-day. For a detailed review, see the Fairline Targa 43 for hire.
  5. RIB or speedboat, for thrill-seekers fishing or exploring caves; up to 6 guests; from €350 half-day.

When to go and do you need a licence?

The best season is October to April, when the Shamal wind is mild and temperatures are pleasant. From May to September, temperatures exceed 40°C and the Shamal can create choppy conditions.

For a quieter experience, visit in March or November; the sea is calm in Khor Ash Sham and dolphins are abundant, but avoid the July Shamal if you are prone to seasickness.

On licences: Omani law requires an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) for bareboat charter of vessels over a certain size. For dayboats, many operators do not require a licence if a skipper is included. Always confirm exact requirements with the operator when booking; they will advise on whether you need to bring documentation. If you are new to boat rental, you can read how boat rental works on the website.

At a glance

  • Price band: €40 to €1,200 per person/day
  • Peak months: November to March
  • Trip length: 4 to 8 hours typical
  • Capacity: 2 to 20 guests
  • Currency: EUR (€)
  • Licence: ICC for bareboat; operator may provide skipper

Questions about boat rental in Musandam

Can I charter a boat for a day without a licence?

Yes, most charter companies in Musandam include a captain and crew, so no licence is needed for the passenger.

What is the best way to see dolphins in Musandam?

Dolphin-watching is best on a morning dhow cruise in Khor Ash Sham, with sightings guaranteed on most trips from October to April.

Are there any hidden costs when renting a boat in Musandam?

Fuel is usually included in the price for day charters, but check whether a tourism fee or park entry fee applies at Khasab.

Can I rent a boat for a multi-day trip along the Musandam coast?

Yes, several operators offer liveaboard charters to remote beaches like Kumzar, though advance booking is required.
